item.title: Staff Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Drelic, a few things:

1. In the future, you can copy text from the page source button at the bottom of pages to avoid issues like this.

2. If you're reverting an accidental change immediately, you don't need to announce it to the world.

3. Please don't reply to your own posts. It's against the rules, and there's a perfectly good edit button beneath each post's options menu.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: OH MY GOD SORRY I WAS COPYING THE IMAGE MODULE AND I EDITED IT I WILL CHANGE IT BACK

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How does the Foundation know that the tentacle and the guy are really separate (aside from the brain death thing)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My interpretations,

Why isn't the SCP referred to as SCP-2xxx instead of Jacob?

This thing can smash walls even when 'sedated.' So not upsetting it is probably best for continued containment. As to why it wasn't stated plainly— maybe it was meant to be inferred? That's my best guess.

Finally, the SCP/Jacob mentions fighting terrorists and monsters, much like the MTFs. It shouldn't have that knowledge of the Foundation.

It know it isn't 'normal' and the Foundation are studying it. Logic would suggest other anomalies exist which the Foundation would be interested in.

I also noticed that the age was sixteen, but his mind seemed to be like that of a ten or twelve year old.

If I read this right, the one doing the talking/thinking is the object connecting through Jacob. Maybe it is 10-12 wherever its from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I came into this expecting x- men but was instead pleasantly surprised. Good writing technique and great addenda! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a skilful blend of body horror and mind screw. The image of the SCP trying to emerge (?) fully and wrecking everything in reach, alternating with the poor schlub teenager who wants to be a superhero - and his parents have been mindwiped to forget him?

For some reason, I imagined the appendage as being invisible, which somehow made it worse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kind of liked the character in the interview logs, to me, he simply read like a well-meaning, mild-mannered individual (a little dated, but seemed like a teenager straight out of the 50s), so it worked for me. I'll lend my +1 to this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A great SCP, but I have some criticisms of the interview logs. Why is Dr. Bauer referred to as Mr. Johnson by the SCP? Is it a revision error, a pseudonym, or does it have something to do with the SCP? I did notice that the Dr. told it to call him Frank. Why isn't the SCP referred to as SCP-2xxx instead of Jacob? Is the arm an SCP, but not the person? You could also just say that it is preferable to use Jacob for psychological reasons. Finally, the SCP/Jacob mentions fighting terrorists and monsters, much like the MTFs. It shouldn't have that knowledge of the Foundation. I also noticed that the age was sixteen, but his mind seemed to be like that of a ten or twelve year old. Aside from those questions, this is a great SCP.
+1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember this from the first time around; happy to see it polished and back up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Image from wikimedia commons

Many thanks to all the people who've given feedback on this, whether it was a detailed read-through or a cursory glance.
